1033 Changed speed to 5 knots, 39 rpm. Entering belt of brash ice, 8/10 coverage.. 1042 Entered open water.
1306 Entered ice pack, steering various courses at various speeds through the ice, 6/10 to 10/10 coverage. Ice hummocked and rafted.
1445 Ship held in pressure ridge, shifted to combination CHARLIE using engines #5 and 6, ahead 25 rpm to keep screws clear. Lead closed astern.

1702 Ice surrounding ship is beginning to ease in pressure.. 1717 Underway, commenced maneuvering to clear ice floe.. 1730 Broke free of floe, entered lead of 2/10 coverage of brash ice, maneuvering in a westerly direction to join U.S.S. NESPELEN (AOG-55) at 4 miles distant.
1805 Eased into ice floe 1000 yards from NESPELEN, stopped all engines and lying to, to await improvement in ice conditions before proceeding.

2205 Ship drifted clear of ice.. 2210 Maneuvering on various courses and speeds to lodge ship in the ice.. 2226 Entered ice consisting of giant puddled floe, 2 feet thick. Maneuvering as necessary to lodge ship in ice.
0-4 Lying to in ice off Resolute Bay, Cornwallis Island, Canadian N.W.T.. 0-4 Awaiting more favorable ice conditions before attempting transit with NESPELEN.
Note: 0-4: Lying to in ice off Resolute Bay, Cornwallis Island, Canadian N.W.T.

Ice_Log: 0905 Commenced maneuvering on various courses and speeds investigating ice conditions to the east of Resolute Bay, Cornwallis Island, Canadian  N.W.T.. 0945 Decided ice conditions would permit escorting the U.S.S. NESPELEN (AOG-55) to open water.
Note: 9:05 undereway

Ice_Log: 1005 Formation distance will vary depending on ice conditions.. 1017 Maneuvering on easterly courses through the ice.. 1035 Following leads between medium and giant puddled floes.
Note: He goes east then west back to start point.

Ice_Log: 12-16 Underway as before, steeering base course 100 t, using various speeds changing course to right and left to avoid ice.

Note: 1325: Cape Hotham abeam to port, distant 3.1 miles.

Ice_Log: 1540 Reduced speed to 10 knots in scattered ice.

1604 Commenced maneuvering on general easterly headings at various speeds while passing through ice fields.. 4PM to 8PM Ice concentration varied throughout watch from 1/10 to 5/10 coverage small medium floes with large areas of brash and block.
2114 Entered open water.
0325 Entered area of scattered ice, reduced speed to 10 knots, 79 rpm.
0419 Entering ice fields 4/10 coverage, changed formation speed to 8 knots, 63 rpm, maneuvering to avoid ice contacts.

0005 Entered open water. 0511 With decreasing ice coverage, changed speed to 10 knots, 79 rpm.. 0520 Entered open water, ice coverage less than 1/10, increased formation speed to 13 knots, 104 rpm, maneuvering as necessary to avoid scattered blocks, growlers, and bergy bits.
0654 Entered belt of ice, commenced maneuvering at various courses and speeds.
0738 Clear of ice, ceased maneuvering set course 116 t, 115 pgc, speed 13 knots, 104 rpm.
0900 Passing through belt of ice 2/10 coverage, brash and growlers.. 0909 Entered open water.. 0953 Entered belt of ice, 2/10 coverage, brash and growlers.
1000 Entered open water.

Note: 0200: Radar contact on Wolstenholm Island bearing 035 t, distant approximately 35 miles.

Ice_Log: 0448 Commenced maneuvering at various courses to avoid numerous ice bergs, bergy bits and growlers.

Note: 0600: Entered North Star Bay, commenced maneuvering to approach anchorage.
